AddOrSubtractFromDate.description: Returns a DateTime offset by N seconds/minutes/hours/days/months/years from an input date.
AddOrSubtractFromDate.use_when: User wants date arithmetic; for formatting a date use FormatDateAsText; for parsing a string into DateTime use ExtractDateTime.

AddTransactionItem.description: Adds a new transaction item to an Orchestrator queue inside a long-running job context.
AddTransactionItem.use_when: User wants to enqueue mid-process; for dispatcher-style bulk loading use BulkAddQueueItems.

AppendLine.description: Appends a line of text to an existing text file, creating it if missing.
AppendLine.use_when: User wants incremental log/audit writes; for one-shot text writes use Write Text File.

Beep.description: Plays a system beep at a given frequency and duration.
Beep.use_when: User wants an audible cue (developer/test only); avoid in unattended automations.

BeginProcess.description: Marks the start of a logical process for tracking and analytics in Insights / Process Mining.
BeginProcess.use_when: User wants to time a logical process span; pair with the corresponding end-of-process tracking.

ChangeCase.description: Returns a string converted to upper, lower, or title case.
ChangeCase.use_when: User wants a case transformation; for find/replace use Replace; for trim/split use ModifyText.

CheckFalse.description: Asserts that a condition is False, throwing on True.
CheckFalse.use_when: User wants an inline negative assertion; for truthy assertion use CheckTrue; in tests prefer the Verify* family.

CheckTrue.description: Asserts that a condition is True, throwing on False.
CheckTrue.use_when: User wants an inline positive assertion; for negative assertion use CheckFalse; in tests prefer the Verify* family.

ClearDataTable.description: Removes all rows from a DataTable while preserving its column schema.
ClearDataTable.use_when: User wants to reset a DataTable for reuse; to drop the schema entirely just rebuild with BuildDataTable.

CombineText.description: Concatenates multiple strings with an optional separator.
CombineText.use_when: User wants to join strings (String.Join equivalent in activity form); for in-place expression use VB's String.Join.

CompressFiles.description: Compresses one or more files / folders into a ZIP archive.
CompressFiles.use_when: User wants to bundle artifacts for upload or email; for extraction use ExtractFiles.

CopyFolderX.description: Copies a folder (recursive) from source to destination, optionally overwriting.
CopyFolderX.use_when: User wants to duplicate a folder tree; for a single file use Copy File; for moving (not copying) use MoveFolderX.

CreateFile.description: Creates an empty file at the given path.
CreateFile.use_when: User wants a placeholder file; to write content use Write Text File or AppendLine.

CustomInput.description: Displays a custom HTML form dialog and captures the user's response.
CustomInput.use_when: User wants a richer prompt than InputDialog; for simple text input use InputDialog.

Delete.description: Deletes a file or folder at the given path.
Delete.use_when: User wants to remove a filesystem entry; for ZIP-and-delete patterns use CompressFiles then Delete.

DeleteFolderX.use_when: User wants to remove a directory tree; for single files use Delete.

DeleteQueueItems.description: Deletes queue items from an Orchestrator queue by ID list or filter.
DeleteQueueItems.use_when: User wants to purge stale or successful items; pair with GetQueueItems to enumerate first.

DeleteStorageFile.description: Deletes a file from an Orchestrator Storage Bucket.
DeleteStorageFile.use_when: User wants to remove a bucket-stored file; pair with ListStorageFiles to enumerate first.

DisableTrigger.description: Disables an Orchestrator trigger by name.
DisableTrigger.use_when: User wants to pause scheduled or queue-driven triggers temporarily; re-enable with EnableTrigger.

DownloadFileFromUrl.description: Downloads a file from an HTTP(S) URL to a local path.
DownloadFileFromUrl.use_when: User wants a file fetched by URL; for authenticated APIs use NetHttpRequest with response-to-file.

DownloadStorageFile.description: Downloads a file from an Orchestrator Storage Bucket to a local path.
DownloadStorageFile.use_when: User wants a bucket-stored file locally; pair with UploadStorageFile for the inverse.

EnableTrigger.description: Enables a previously disabled Orchestrator trigger by name.
EnableTrigger.use_when: User wants to resume a paused trigger; pair with DisableTrigger.

EvaluateBusinessRule.description: Evaluates a Business Rule from the Rules Catalog and returns its outcome / actions.
EvaluateBusinessRule.use_when: User wants to delegate decisioning to a centrally-managed rule; for inline VB conditions use If.

ExecutePowerShell.description: Runs a PowerShell script and captures its output.
ExecutePowerShell.use_when: User wants to invoke Windows-side admin tasks; prefer dedicated activities (filesystem/registry) when one exists.

ExtractDateTime.description: Parses a string into a DateTime using a culture-aware format pattern.
ExtractDateTime.use_when: User wants to convert a string to DateTime; for the inverse use FormatDateAsText.

ExtractFiles.description: Extracts the contents of a ZIP archive to a target folder.
ExtractFiles.use_when: User wants to unpack a ZIP; for compression use CompressFiles.

ExtractText.description: Extracts a substring from a source string by regex or position.
ExtractText.use_when: User wants a piece of a larger string; for find/replace use Replace; for splitting use SplitText.

FileChangeTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a watched file is created/modified/deleted.
FileChangeTrigger.use_when: User wants to react to filesystem changes; v3 (FileChangeTriggerV3) is the latest stable.

FileChangeTriggerV2.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

FileChangeTriggerV2.description: v2 of FileChangeTrigger — superseded by FileChangeTriggerV3 in modern projects.
FileChangeTriggerV2.use_when: User has a legacy spec referencing v2; new specs should use FileChangeTriggerV3.

FileChangeTriggerV3.description: v3 of FileChangeTrigger — current stable file-watch trigger with extended event types.
FileChangeTriggerV3.use_when: User wants to react to filesystem changes in a modern project; v1/v2 remain for backwards compatibility only.

FileExistsX.description: Returns True if a file exists at the given path.
FileExistsX.use_when: User wants a simple existence check; for folders use FolderExistsX.

FindAndReplace.description: Finds and replaces text in a file in place, optionally using regex.
FindAndReplace.use_when: User wants to mutate file contents; for in-memory string replacement use Replace.

FolderExistsX.description: Returns True if a folder exists at the given path.
FolderExistsX.use_when: User wants a simple folder existence check; for files use FileExistsX.

ForEachFolderX.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ForEachFolderX.description: Iterates over folders matching a pattern under a root directory.
ForEachFolderX.use_when: User wants to walk a directory tree of folders; for files use ForEachFileX; for in-memory collections use ForEach.

FormatDateAsText.description: Formats a DateTime as a string using a culture-aware format pattern.
FormatDateAsText.use_when: User wants a stringified date; for parsing the inverse use ExtractDateTime.

FormatValue.description: Formats a numeric value using a format pattern (currency, decimal, percent).
FormatValue.use_when: User wants a stringified number with explicit formatting; VB's String.Format suffices in expressions.

GetCurrentJobInfo.description: Returns Orchestrator metadata about the currently-running job (id, robot, folder).
GetCurrentJobInfo.use_when: User wants to read job-context properties for logging or correlation.

GetEnvironmentFolder.description: Returns the path of a Windows special folder (Desktop, MyDocuments, etc.).
GetEnvironmentFolder.use_when: User wants to write to a known user folder; for an environment variable use GetEnvironmentVariable.

GetEnvironmentVariable.description: Returns the value of a process-, user-, or machine-scoped environment variable.
GetEnvironmentVariable.use_when: User wants to read a configured env var; for setting use SetEnvironmentVariable.

GetFileInfoX.description: Returns metadata (size, created, modified) for a file as a FileInfo object.
GetFileInfoX.use_when: User wants file metadata for routing decisions; for folder metadata use GetFolderInfoX.

GetFolderInfoX.description: Returns metadata (size, created, modified) for a folder as a DirectoryInfo object.
GetFolderInfoX.use_when: User wants folder metadata; for file metadata use GetFileInfoX.

GetJobs.description: Queries Orchestrator for jobs matching given filters.
GetJobs.use_when: User wants to enumerate or correlate Orchestrator jobs; for the current job only use GetCurrentJobInfo.

GetLastDownloadedFile.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

GetLastDownloadedFile.description: Returns the path of the most recently downloaded file from the active browser context.
GetLastDownloadedFile.use_when: User wants to capture a download triggered by a browser interaction; pair with NClick on the download link.

GetProcesses.description: Returns a list of currently-running Windows processes.
GetProcesses.use_when: User wants to enumerate processes for diagnostics or detect a running app; to terminate one use KillProcess.

GetQueueItems.description: Queries Orchestrator for queue items matching given filters (status, reference, dates).
GetQueueItems.use_when: User wants to inspect or audit queue contents; for popping the next work item use GetQueueItem.

GetRowItem.description: Reads a single column value from a DataRow by column name or index.
GetRowItem.use_when: User wants a typed cell value from a DataRow; usually the inline expression `row("Col")` is more idiomatic.

GetSecret.description: Reads a secret value from a configured secret store (Orchestrator credentials, vault, etc.).
GetSecret.use_when: User wants a secret without storing it in code or config; for credential pairs use GetRobotCredential.

GetTransactionItem.description: Pops the next transaction from a queue, mirroring REFramework's Get Transaction Data step.
GetTransactionItem.use_when: User is hand-authoring a Performer; in REFramework this is wired automatically.

GetUsernamePasswordX.description: Reads a username/password pair from a Windows Credential Manager entry by name.
GetUsernamePasswordX.use_when: User wants OS-level credentials; for Orchestrator-managed credentials use GetRobotCredential.

GlobalVariableChangedTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a workflow Global Variable's value changes.
GlobalVariableChangedTrigger.use_when: User wants reactive flow on global state mutations; pair with NotifyGlobalVariableChanged from the producer side.

InterruptibleDoWhile.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

InterruptibleDoWhile.description: DoWhile loop that yields to interrupts (Stop, signals) at safe checkpoints between iterations.
InterruptibleDoWhile.use_when: User wants long-running iteration that honors cooperative cancellation; for non-interruptible loops use DoWhile.

InterruptibleWhile.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

InterruptibleWhile.description: While loop that yields to interrupts at safe checkpoints between iterations.
InterruptibleWhile.use_when: User wants long-running iteration with cooperative cancellation; for non-interruptible loops use While.

InvokeComMethod.description: Invokes a COM method on a registered COM object (e.g. Office automation).
InvokeComMethod.use_when: User wants to call COM-based APIs; for VB.NET methods use InvokeMethod; for inline code use InvokeCode.

InvokeProcess.description: Launches an external Windows process and optionally waits for its exit / captures stdout.
InvokeProcess.use_when: User wants to run a CLI tool or executable; for PowerShell scripts use ExecutePowerShell.

InvokeVBScript.description: Runs a VBScript file with optional arguments and captures its output.
InvokeVBScript.use_when: User has a legacy .vbs script to call; for new scripting prefer ExecutePowerShell or InvokeCode.

InvokeWorkflowInteractive.description: Invokes a subworkflow in an interactive session (Picture-in-Picture / unattended elevation).
InvokeWorkflowInteractive.use_when: User wants to run a child workflow with elevated UI access; for standard subworkflow calls use InvokeWorkflowFile.

IsMatch.description: Returns True if a string matches a regex pattern.
IsMatch.use_when: User wants a boolean regex check; for capturing matches use Matches; VB's Regex.IsMatch suffices in expressions.

ListStorageFiles.description: Lists files in an Orchestrator Storage Bucket, optionally filtered by prefix.
ListStorageFiles.use_when: User wants to enumerate bucket contents; pair with DownloadStorageFile / DeleteStorageFile.

ManualTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when invoked manually (debug / dev-time only).
ManualTrigger.use_when: User wants a hand-fired trigger for testing; production should use FileChange/Time/Queue triggers.

Matches.description: Returns the list of regex matches against a string.
Matches.use_when: User wants captured matches; for a boolean check use IsMatch; VB's Regex.Matches suffices in expressions.

ModifyDate.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ModifyDate.description: Returns a DateTime with selected components replaced (year, month, day, hour, etc.).
ModifyDate.use_when: User wants component-level date editing; for offset-based shifts use AddOrSubtractFromDate.

ModifyText.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ModifyText.description: Applies a sequence of text transformations (trim, replace, case, encode) in one activity.
ModifyText.use_when: User wants several string transforms in one step; for a single transform pick the specific activity (Replace, ChangeCase, etc.).

MoveFolderX.description: Moves a folder (recursive) from source to destination.
MoveFolderX.use_when: User wants to relocate a folder; for non-destructive copy use CopyFolderX.

NotifyGlobalVariableChanged.description: Signals to subscribers that a Global Variable's value changed.
NotifyGlobalVariableChanged.use_when: User wants to manually fire a change notification; pair with GlobalVariableChangedTrigger on the consumer side.

OrchestratorHttpRequest.description: Performs an authenticated HTTP request against the Orchestrator API.
OrchestratorHttpRequest.use_when: User wants Orchestrator API calls without managing auth headers; for arbitrary REST APIs use NetHttpRequest.

fixed_attrs.Description: This placeholder activity will be replaced at build time and can only be used within an execution template.

Placeholder.description: Empty placeholder activity used as a generator stub during scaffolding.
Placeholder.use_when: User wants to mark a TODO spot in a workflow; should be replaced with the real activity before publish.

PostponeTransactionItem.description: Reschedules a queue transaction item to a later deadline.
PostponeTransactionItem.use_when: User wants to defer a transaction (rate-limit, retry-window); for outright failure use SetTransactionStatus(Failed).

ProcessEndTrigger.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ProcessEndTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a Windows process exits.
ProcessEndTrigger.use_when: User wants reactive flow on process termination; v2 (ProcessEndTriggerV2) is the latest stable.

ProcessEndTriggerV2.description: v2 of ProcessEndTrigger — current stable process-exit trigger.
ProcessEndTriggerV2.use_when: User wants modern process-end watching; v1 remains for backwards compatibility.

ProcessStartTrigger.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ProcessStartTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a Windows process starts.
ProcessStartTrigger.use_when: User wants reactive flow on process launch; v2 (ProcessStartTriggerV2) is the latest stable.

ProcessStartTriggerV2.description: v2 of ProcessStartTrigger — current stable process-start trigger.
ProcessStartTriggerV2.use_when: User wants modern process-start watching; v1 remains for backwards compatibility.

ProcessTrackingScope.description: Scope wrapper that emits Process Mining tracking events for activities executed within.
ProcessTrackingScope.use_when: User wants Process Mining instrumentation; for ad-hoc analytics emission use TrackObject.

QueueTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a queue item is added to a watched Orchestrator queue.
QueueTrigger.use_when: User wants reactive Performers; for time-based use TimeTrigger.

RaiseAlert.description: Raises a Process Insights alert with a severity / message.
RaiseAlert.use_when: User wants to surface a problem to ops dashboards; for plain log writes use LogMessage.

ReadStorageText.description: Reads the text content of a file in an Orchestrator Storage Bucket.
ReadStorageText.use_when: User wants bucket-stored text without downloading first; for binary or large files use DownloadStorageFile.

RemoveDataRow.description: Removes a row from a DataTable by index or DataRow object.
RemoveDataRow.use_when: User wants to drop one row; for many-row removal by criteria use FilterDataTable.

RenameFileX.description: Renames a file (or moves it) preserving content.
RenameFileX.use_when: User wants to change a file's name; for moving across drives use Move File.

RenameFolderX.description: Renames a folder (or moves it) preserving content.
RenameFolderX.use_when: User wants to change a folder's name; for moving across drives use MoveFolderX.

RepeatNumberOfTimesX.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

RepeatNumberOfTimesX.description: Loops a fixed number of times.
RepeatNumberOfTimesX.use_when: User wants N-iteration repetition; for collection iteration use ForEach; for condition-driven use While.

RepeatTrigger.description: Trigger that fires on a fixed cadence relative to a base time.
RepeatTrigger.use_when: User wants periodic execution (e.g. every 5 minutes); for cron-style absolute times use TimeTrigger.

Replace.description: Replaces occurrences of a substring or regex with a replacement value.
Replace.use_when: User wants string find/replace; for file in-place edits use FindAndReplace; VB's Replace / Regex.Replace suffices in expressions.

ReportStatus.description: Reports an interim status for the running job to Orchestrator dashboards.
ReportStatus.use_when: User wants progress visibility on long jobs; for terminal status use SetTransactionStatus.

ResetTimer.description: Resets a named Timer activity's elapsed counter to zero.
ResetTimer.use_when: User wants to restart a timing measurement; pair with StartTimer / StopTimer.

ResumeTimer.use_when: User wants to continue a paused measurement; pair with StartTimer / StopTimer / ResetTimer.

Return.description: Returns from the current workflow / scope, propagating an optional value.
Return.use_when: User wants an early exit from a subworkflow; for terminating the entire job use TerminateWorkflow.

RunJob.description: Triggers a job run against an Orchestrator process and optionally waits for completion.
RunJob.use_when: User wants to invoke another process from the current workflow; for fire-and-forget use StartJob.

SelectFile.description: Opens an OS file-picker dialog and returns the chosen path.
SelectFile.use_when: User wants user-driven file selection (attended only); unattended runs should source paths from config.

SelectFolder.description: Opens an OS folder-picker dialog and returns the chosen path.
SelectFolder.use_when: User wants user-driven folder selection (attended only); unattended runs should source paths from config.

SendEmailNotification.description: Sends a templated email notification (Orchestrator-configured SMTP) to one or more recipients.
SendEmailNotification.use_when: User wants ops-style notification email; for full mail control use SendOutlookMail / SendSMTPMail.

SetAsset.description: Writes a value to a named Orchestrator asset.
SetAsset.use_when: User wants to update a centrally-managed value; for credentials use SetCredential.

SetCredential.description: Writes a username/password pair to a named Orchestrator credential asset.
SetCredential.use_when: User wants to rotate or seed a credential asset; for non-credential values use SetAsset.

SetEnvironmentVariable.description: Sets a process- or user-scoped environment variable.
SetEnvironmentVariable.use_when: User wants to inject env state for downstream tools; reads use GetEnvironmentVariable.

SetSecret.description: Writes a secret value to a configured secret store.
SetSecret.use_when: User wants to seed or rotate a secret; reads use GetSecret.

SetTaskStatus.description: Updates the status of a tracked task (Tasks app integration).
SetTaskStatus.use_when: User wants to mark a task complete / cancelled; for queue items use SetTransactionStatus.

SetTraceStatus.description: Sets a trace breadcrumb for distributed tracing of the current job.
SetTraceStatus.use_when: User wants OpenTelemetry-style traces in Insights; for ad-hoc logs use LogMessage.

SetTransactionProgress.description: Updates the progress percentage of a long-running queue transaction.
SetTransactionProgress.use_when: User wants progress visibility on a transaction; for terminal status use SetTransactionStatus.

SetTransactionStatus.description: Marks a queue transaction Successful / Failed (with reason) at the end of processing.
SetTransactionStatus.use_when: User wants to close out a Performer transaction; in REFramework the End Transaction state wires this automatically.

SplitText.description: Splits a string into a list of substrings on a delimiter or regex.
SplitText.use_when: User wants tokenized string output; for joining the inverse use CombineText.

StartJob.description: Triggers a job run against an Orchestrator process and returns immediately (fire-and-forget).
StartJob.use_when: User wants asynchronous process invocation; for synchronous use RunJob.

StartTimer.description: Starts a named Timer activity for measuring elapsed time.
StartTimer.use_when: User wants to measure how long a region takes; pair with StopTimer / ResetTimer.

StartTriggers.description: Starts all (or named) Orchestrator triggers attached to the current process.
StartTriggers.use_when: User wants to enable a trigger set; pair with StopTriggers.

StopJob.description: Stops a running Orchestrator job by ID (cooperative).
StopJob.use_when: User wants to abort a running job from another job; the target job should call ShouldStop at safe points.

StopTimer.description: Stops a named Timer activity and returns the elapsed time.
StopTimer.use_when: User wants to capture a region's duration; pair with StartTimer.

StopTriggers.description: Stops all (or named) Orchestrator triggers attached to the current process.
StopTriggers.use_when: User wants to pause a trigger set; pair with StartTriggers.

TextToLeftRight.description: Splits a string at the first occurrence of a delimiter into (left, right) parts.
TextToLeftRight.use_when: User wants a one-shot split into two pieces; for n-way splits use SplitText.

TimeTrigger.description: Trigger that fires at scheduled times (cron-style).
TimeTrigger.use_when: User wants scheduled execution; for relative cadence use RepeatTrigger.

TimeoutScope.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

TimeoutScope.description: Aborts the activities inside the scope after a configurable timeout.
TimeoutScope.use_when: User wants a hard upper bound on a region's duration; for retry-with-timeout use RetryScope.

TrackObject.description: Emits an analytics event with a named object payload to Insights / Process Mining.
TrackObject.use_when: User wants to record a typed event for analytics; for whole-process spans use ProcessTrackingScope.

TriggerScope.description: Scope wrapper hosting one or more triggers and their reactive bodies.
TriggerScope.use_when: User wants to author trigger-driven workflows; the scope manages start/stop lifecycle.

UpdateRowItem.description: Sets a single column value on a DataRow.
UpdateRowItem.use_when: User wants to mutate one cell in a DataTable; the inline expression `row("Col") = value` is also idiomatic.

UploadStorageFile.description: Uploads a local file to an Orchestrator Storage Bucket.
UploadStorageFile.use_when: User wants to publish artifacts to a bucket; pair with DownloadStorageFile / ListStorageFiles.

WaitQueueItem.description: Waits until a matching item appears on a watched Orchestrator queue.
WaitQueueItem.use_when: User wants to block until queue input is available; for non-blocking polling use GetQueueItems.

WriteStorageText.description: Writes a text payload to a file in an Orchestrator Storage Bucket.
WriteStorageText.use_when: User wants to persist generated text directly to a bucket; for binary or large files use UploadStorageFile.
